debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
f : U → V
g : V → U
h : g ∘ f = id
⊢ image g ∘ image f = id

proof:
by
  have h1 : ∀ (a : U), g (f a) = a := by
    intro a
    have h2 : (g ∘ f) a = a := by
      rw [h]
      <;> rfl
    simp [Function.comp_apply] at h2 ⊢
    <;> exact h2
  
  have h2 : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) (image f s) ⊆ s := by
    intro s t ht
    -- If t is in the image of g, then there exists some b in image f s such that g b = t
    rcases ht with ⟨b, hb, rfl⟩
    -- Now, b is in image f s, so there exists some a in s such that f a = b
    rcases hb with ⟨a, ha, rfl⟩
    -- We have g (f a) = a by h1, so t = a is in s
    have h₃ : g (f a) = a := h1 a
    aesop
  
  have h3 : ∀ (s : Set U), s ⊆ (image g) (image f s) := by
    intro s a ha
    -- For any a in s, we need to show that a is in (image g) (image f s).
    -- Since a ∈ s, f a ∈ image f s. Then, g (f a) ∈ image g (image f s).
    -- But g (f a) = a by h1, so a ∈ image g (image f s).
    have h₄ : f a ∈ image f s := by
      -- f a is in the image of f applied to s because a is in s.
      exact Set.mem_image_of_mem f ha
    have h₅ : g (f a) ∈ (image g) (image f s) := by
      -- Since f a is in image f s, g (f a) is in image g (image f s).
      exact Set.mem_image_of_mem g h₄
    have h₆ : g (f a) = a := h1 a
    -- But g (f a) = a, so a is in image g (image f s).
    rw [h₆] at h₅
    exact h₅
  
  have h4 : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) (image f s) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove (image g) (image f s) ⊆ s using h2
      exact h2 s
    · -- Prove s ⊆ (image g) (image f s) using h3
      exact h3 s
  
  have h5 : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext s
    have h6 : (image g) (image f s) = s := h4 s
    simp [h6]
    <;> aesop
  
  rw [h5]
failed_attempts:
by
            rw [h₁]
        _ = Φ2 (f ⊔ (a : α)) := by
            congr 1 <;>
            ext a <;>
            rfl
        _ = Φ2 (b a) := by
            simp [ext]
        _ = d a := by
            have h₆ := h₂ (a : α)
            have h₇ := h₄ (a : α)
            have h₈ := h₂ (a : α)
            simp_all [CoeMorphisms, Φ1, Φ2, Ψ₁, Ψ₂, Ψ₃, Commute, CommuteWith, CDiagram]
            <;>
            aesop

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext S
    have h₂ : (image g) (image f S) = S := by
      -- Prove that the image of the image of S under f under g is S
      have h₃ : g ∘ f = id := h
      have h₄ : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
        intro x
        have h₅ : (g ∘ f) x = id x := by
          rw [h₃]
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₅ ⊢
        exact h₅
      -- Use the property that g ∘ f = id to show that the image is preserved
      ext x
      simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in the image of g applied to the image of f applied to S, then x is in S
        rintro ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, hy⟩, hxy⟩
        have h₅ : g (f z) = z := h₄ z
        have h₆ : y = g (f z) := by
          calc
            y = g (f z) := by
              simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
              <;> aesop
            _ = g (f z) := rfl
        have h₇ : x = z := by
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;> aesop
        simp_all
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in S, then x is in the image of g applied to the image of f applied to S
        rintro hx
        have h₅ : f x ∈ image f S := by
          -- f x is in the image of f applied to S
          exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
        have h₆ : g (f x) = x := h₄ x
        have h₇ : x ∈ (image g) (image f S) := by
          -- x is in the image of g applied to the image of f applied to S
          refine' ⟨f x, h₅, _⟩
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₇
    -- Use the above result to conclude that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id
    simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g (image f s) : Set U) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove that image g (image f s) ⊆ s
      intro x hx
      -- If x is in image g (image f s), then there exists y in image f s such that g y = x
      rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
      -- Since y is in image f s, there exists z in s such that f z = y
      rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
      -- Now, g (f z) = z because g ∘ f = id
      have h₂ : g (f z) = z := by
        have h₃ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h₃
      -- Therefore, x = z is in s
      exact hz
    · -- Prove that s ⊆ image g (image f s)
      intro x hx
      -- For any x in s, f x is in image f s
      have h₂ : f x ∈ image f s := by
        exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
      -- Then, g (f x) = x, so x is in image g (image f s)
      have h₃ : g (f x) = x := by
        have h₄ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h₄
      refine' ⟨f x, h₂, _⟩
      simp [h₃]
  
  ext s
  rw [h₁ s]
  <;> simp

by
  funext s
  ext x
  simp only [Function.comp_apply, image_image, image_id, h]
  <;>
  simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
  <;>
  aesop

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    apply funext
    intro s
    have h₂ : (image g) (image f s) = s := by
      have h₃ : (image g) (image f s) = image g (image f s) := rfl
      rw [h₃]
      have h₄ : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ s → g (f x) = x := by
        intro x hx
        have h₅ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) = id := h
          rw [h₆]
          <;> simp [hx]
        simpa using h₅
      have h₅ : image g (image f s) = s := by
        apply Set.Subset.antisymm
        · -- Prove image g (image f s) ⊆ s
          intro x hx
          rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
          rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
          have h₆ : g (f z) = z := h₄ z hz
          simp_all
        · -- Prove s ⊆ image g (image f s)
          intro x hx
          have h₆ : x ∈ s := hx
          have h₇ : f x ∈ image f s := by
            apply mem_image_of_mem
            exact h₆
          have h₈ : g (f x) = x := h₄ x h₆
          have h₉ : x = g (f x) := by rw [h₈]
          have h₁₀ : g (f x) ∈ image g (image f s) := by
            apply mem_image_of_mem
            exact h₇
          rw [h₉] at *
          exact h₁₀
      exact h₅
    simpa using h₂
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (x : U), g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₂ : (g ∘ f) x = (id : U → U) x := by rw [h]
    have h₃ : (g ∘ f) x = g (f x) := rfl
    have h₄ : (id : U → U) x = x := rfl
    rw [h₃, h₄] at h₂
    exact h₂
  
  have h₂ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) (image f s) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    simp only [Set.mem_image, exists_prop]
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in (image g) (image f s), then x is in s
      rintro ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
      -- y is in image f s, so there exists z in s such that f z = y
      rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
      -- Now we have g (f z) = x and h₁ tells us g (f z) = z
      have h₃ : g (f z) = z := h₁ z
      linarith
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in s, then x is in (image g) (image f s)
      rintro hz
      -- Since x is in s, f x is in image f s
      have h₃ : f x ∈ image f s := by
        exact ⟨x, hz, rfl⟩
      -- And g (f x) = x, so x is in (image g) (image f s)
      have h₄ : g (f x) = x := h₁ x
      exact ⟨f x, h₃, by simp [h₄]⟩
  
  -- Now we can use h₂ to prove that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id
  ext s
  have h₃ := h₂ s
  simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
  <;> aesop

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), image g (image f s) = s := by
    intro s
    have h₂ : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ s → g (f x) = x := by
      intro x hx
      have h₃ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
        rw [h]
        <;> simp
      simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₃
      exact h₃
    -- Prove that image g (image f s) = s
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove that image g (image f s) ⊆ s
      intro x hx
      rw [Set.mem_image] at hx
      rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
      rw [Set.mem_image] at hy
      rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, hyz⟩
      have h₄ : g (f z) = z := h₂ z hz
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Prove that s ⊆ image g (image f s)
      intro x hx
      have h₃ : f x ∈ image f s := Set.mem_image_of_mem f hx
      have h₄ : g (f x) = x := h₂ x hx
      have h₅ : x ∈ image g (image f s) := by
        refine' ⟨f x, _, by simp [h₄]⟩
        exact h₃
      exact h₅
  -- Extend the result to the entire function
  funext s
  have h₂ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext s
    rw [Function.comp_apply]
    rw [h₁ s]
  simp_all

sorry
  sorry

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    ext s
    -- We need to show that (image g) (image f s) = s for any subset s of U.
    -- This is equivalent to showing that g (f '' s) = s.
    have h₂ : (image g) (image f s) = s := by
      -- Use the fact that g ∘ f = id to show that g is a right inverse of f.
      have h₃ : g ∘ f = id := h
      have h₄ : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
        intro x
        have h₅ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          simp [h₃]
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₅ ⊢
        exact h₅
      -- Use the right inverse property to show that g (f '' s) = s.
      ext x
      simp only [Set.mem_image]
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ g (f '' s), then x ∈ s.
        rintro ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, hx⟩
        have h₆ : g (f z) = z := h₄ z
        have h₇ : g y = x := by simpa [hx] using hx
        have h₈ : y = f z := by
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;> aesop
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ g (f '' s).
        rintro hx
        have h₉ : g (f x) = x := h₄ x
        refine' ⟨f x, ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩, _⟩
        simp [h₉]
    -- Conclude that (image g) (image f s) = s.
    simp_all
  -- Conclude that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id.
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (x : U), g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₂ : (g ∘ f) x = id x := by
      rw [h]
      <;> simp [Function.funext_iff]
    simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₂
    exact h₂
  
  have h₂ : ∀ (X : Set V), image g (image f X) = X := by
    intro X
    have h₃ : image g (image f X) = X := by
      apply Set.ext
      intro x
      simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in image g (image f X), then x is in X
        intro h₄
        obtain ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, hy⟩, h₅⟩ := h₄
        have h₆ := h₁ z
        have h₇ : g (f z) = z := h₁ z
        have h₈ : y = f z := by simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        have h₉ : g y = x := by simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in X, then x is in image g (image f X)
        intro h₄
        refine' ⟨f x, ⟨x, by simp_all [Function.comp_apply], _⟩, _⟩
        · -- Show that f x is in the image of f
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;> aesop
        · -- Show that g (f x) = x
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;> aesop
    exact h₃
  
  have h₃ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext X
    rw [h₂]
    <;> simp
  
  exact h₃

by
  have h_main : ∀ (A : Set U), (image g) (image f A) = A := by
    intro A
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove (image g) (image f A) ⊆ A
      intro y hy
      -- y ∈ (image g) (image f A)
      rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
      -- z ∈ image f A and g z = y
      rcases hz with ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
      -- x ∈ A and f x = z
      have h₁ : g (f x) = x := by
        have h₂ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        exact h₂
      -- Since g (f x) = x, we have y = x ∈ A
      exact hx
    · -- Prove A ⊆ (image g) (image f A)
      intro y hy
      -- y ∈ A
      have h₁ : (f y : V) ∈ image f A := by
        -- f y ∈ image f A because y ∈ A
        exact ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
      -- g (f y) = y because g ∘ f = id
      have h₂ : g (f y) = y := by
        have h₃ : (g ∘ f) y = y := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        exact h₃
      -- Therefore, y ∈ (image g) (image f A)
      refine' ⟨f y, h₁, _⟩
      <;> simp_all
  
  have h_final : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext A
    rw [Function.comp_apply]
    rw [h_main A]
    <;> simp
  
  apply h_final

by
      -- Use the bijection to show that the set is denumerable
      exact denumerable_of_equiv h2
    exact h3
  exact h1

by
            -- Use the fact that there are infinitely many even integers
            let f : ℕ → {n : Int | even n} := fun n => ⟨2 * n, by
              simp [Int.even_iff]
              <;> ring_nf
              <;> norm_num
              <;> omega⟩
            -- Prove that f is injective
            have h₁ : Function.Injective f := by
              intro n m h₂
              simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
              <;> norm_cast at *
              <;> omega
            -- Use the fact that there is an injection from ℕ to the set to prove it's infinite
            exact Infinite.of_injective f h₁
          exact h
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Prove that the set is Dedekind finite
          -- (This is a placeholder for the actual proof)
          apply inferInstance
        })
    exact this
  exact h

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g ∘ image f) s = s := by
    intro s
    have h₂ : image g (image f s) = s := by
      ext x
      have h₃ : x ∈ image g (image f s) ↔ x ∈ s := by
        constructor
        · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in image g (image f s), then x is in s
          intro hx
          -- Since x is in the image of g, there exists y in V such that g(y) = x
          rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
          -- y is in the image of f, so there exists z in U such that f(z) = y
          rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
          -- Since g ∘ f = id, g(f(z)) = z, so x = z and z ∈ s
          have h₄ : g (f z) = z := by
            have h₅ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
              have h₆ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
                calc
                  (g ∘ f) z = id z := by rw [h]
                  _ = z := by rfl
              exact h₆
            simpa using h₅
          simp_all
        · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in s, then x is in image g (image f s)
          intro hx
          -- Since x is in s, f(x) is in the image of f, and g(f(x)) = x
          have h₃ : f x ∈ image f s := by
            exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
          have h₄ : g (f x) = x := by
            have h₅ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
              calc
                (g ∘ f) x = id x := by rw [h]
                _ = x := by rfl
            simpa using h₅
          -- So x = g(f(x)) is in the image of g
          exact ⟨f x, h₃, by simp [h₄]⟩
      -- By set extensionality, the two sets are equal
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  -- Since (image g ∘ image f) s = s for all s, the composition is the identity
  ext s
  exact h₁ s

by
  have h₁ : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₂ := congr_fun h x
    simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h₂
  ext s
  have h₂ : (image g : Set V → Set U) ((image f : Set U → Set V) s) = s := by
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_preimage]
    constructor
    · intro hx
      obtain ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, rfl⟩ := hx
      have h₃ := h₁ z
      have h₄ : x ∈ s := by
        have h₅ : z ∈ s := hz
        have h₆ : g (f z) = z := h₁ z
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₄
    · intro hx
      have h₃ : x ∈ s := hx
      have h₄ : ∃ (z : U), z ∈ s ∧ g (f z) = x := by
        refine' ⟨x, _⟩
        have h₅ : ∃ (z : U), f z = f x := by
          refine' ⟨x, _⟩
          <;> simp
        obtain ⟨z, hz⟩ := h₅
        have h₆ : g (f z) = z := h₁ z
        have h₇ : z ∈ s := by
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;> aesop
        have h₈ : g (f z) = z := h₁ z
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      obtain ⟨z, hz, hz'⟩ := h₄
      refine' ⟨f z, _⟩
      constructor
      · refine' ⟨z, hz, _⟩
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      · simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
  simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
  <;> aesop

by
  have h_image_comp : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext s
    have h₁ : (image g) (image f s) = s := by
      -- We need to show that applying g's image to f's image of s gives s back.
      -- Since g ∘ f = id, we can use this to show that the images are inverses.
      have h₂ : ∀ x : U, x ∈ s → g (f x) = x := by
        intro x _
        have h₃ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₃ ⊢
        <;> exact h₃
      -- We use the fact that g ∘ f = id to show that g (f x) = x for all x ∈ s.
      -- This will help us show that the images are inverses.
      have h₃ : (image g) (image f s) = s := by
        apply Set.ext
        intro x
        simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        constructor
        · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ (image g) (image f s), then x ∈ s.
          rintro ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
          -- We need to find some z ∈ s such that g (f z) = x.
          -- Using the condition g ∘ f = id, we can find such a z.
          have h₄ : ∃ (z : U), z ∈ s ∧ g (f z) = y := by
            -- Since y ∈ image f s, there exists some z ∈ s such that f z = y.
            obtain ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩ := hy
            refine' ⟨z, hz, _⟩
            -- Since g ∘ f = id, we have g (f z) = z.
            have h₅ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
              rw [h]
              <;> simp
            simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₅ ⊢
            <;> exact h₅
          obtain ⟨z, hz, hz'⟩ := h₄
          have h₅ : g (f z) = y := hz'
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₆ ⊢
          <;> aesop
        · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ (image g) (image f s).
          rintro hx
          -- We need to find some y ∈ image f s such that g y = x.
          -- Since x ∈ s, we can use f x ∈ image f s and g (f x) = x.
          have h₄ : f x ∈ image f s := by
            refine' ⟨x, hx, _⟩
            <;> simp
          refine' ⟨f x, h₄, _⟩
          -- Using g ∘ f = id, we have g (f x) = x.
          have h₅ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₅ ⊢
          <;> aesop
      exact h₃
    simp_all
  exact h_image_comp

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g ∘ image f) s = s := by
    intro s
    have h₂ : (image g ∘ image f) s = image g (image f s) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : image g (image f s) = s := by
      apply Set.ext
      intro x

    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ (image g) (image f s)

    have h₄ : f a ∈ image f s := by
      -- f a is in the image of f applied to s because a is in s.
      exact Set.mem_image_of_mem f ha
    have h₅ : g (f a) ∈ (image g) (image f s) := by
      -- Since f a is in image f s, g (f a) is in image g (image f s).
      exact Set.mem_image_of_mem g h₄
    have h₆ : g (f a) = a := h1 a
    -- But g (f a) = a, so a is in image g (image f s).
    rw [h₆] at h₅
    exact h₅
  
  have h4 : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) (image f s) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove (image g) (image f s) ⊆ s using h2
      exact h2 s
    · -- Prove s ⊆ (image g) (image f s) using h3
      exact h3 s
  
  have h5 : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext s
    have h6 : (image g) (image f s) = s := h4 s
    simp [h6]
    <;> aesop
  
  rw [h5]

by
  have h_main : ∀ (S : Set U), image g (image f S) = S := by
    intro S
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove image g (image f S) ⊆ S
      intro x hx
      -- Obtain y ∈ image f S such that g y = x
      have h1 : ∃ (y : V), y ∈ image f S ∧ g y = x := by
        simpa [Set.mem_image] using hx
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := h1
      -- Obtain z ∈ S such that f z = y
      have h2 : ∃ (z : U), z ∈ S ∧ f z = y := by
        simpa [Set.mem_image] using hy1
      obtain ⟨z, hz1, hz2⟩ := h2
      -- Use the fact that g ∘ f = id to show x = z
      have h3 : g (f z) = z := by
        have h4 : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h4
      have h4 : g y = z := by
        calc
          g y = g (f z) := by rw [hz2]
          _ = z := by rw [h3]
      have h5 : x = z := by
        calc
          x = g y := by rw [hy2]
          _ = z := by rw [h4]
      -- Conclude x ∈ S
      rw [h5]
      exact hz1
    · -- Prove S ⊆ image g (image f S)
      intro x hx
      -- Show that x ∈ image g (image f S)
      have h1 : x ∈ image g (image f S) := by
        -- Since x ∈ S, f x ∈ image f S
        have h2 : f x ∈ image f S := by
          exact Set.mem_image_of_mem f hx
        -- And g (f x) = x, so x ∈ image g (image f S)
        have h3 : g (f x) = x := by
          have h4 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h4
        have h4 : x ∈ image g (image f S) := by
          refine' ⟨f x, h2, _⟩
          rw [h3]
        exact h4
      exact h1
  
  have h_final : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext S
    have h₁ : (image g ∘ image f) S = image g (image f S) := rfl
    rw [h₁]
    have h₂ : image g (image f S) = S := h_main S
    rw [h₂]
    <;> simp [Set.ext_iff]
  
  exact h_final

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (A : Set U), (image g) (image f A) = A := by
    intro A
    have h₂ : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ (image g) (image f A) ↔ x ∈ A := by
      intro x
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in (image g) (image f A), then x is in A
        intro hx
        -- Since x is in (image g) (image f A), there exists y in (image f A) such that g y = x
        have h₃ : ∃ (y : V), y ∈ image f A ∧ g y = x := hx
        obtain ⟨y, hy, hy'⟩ := h₃
        -- Since y is in (image f A), there exists a in A such that f a = y
        have h₄ : ∃ (a : U), a ∈ A ∧ f a = y := hy
        obtain ⟨a, ha, ha'⟩ := h₄
        -- Since g ∘ f = id, g (f a) = a
        have h₅ : g (f a) = a := by
          have h₅₁ : g (f a) = a := by
            have h₅₂ : (g ∘ f) a = a := by
              rw [h]
              <;> simp
            simpa using h₅₂
          exact h₅₁
        -- But g y = x and y = f a, so x = a and a is in A
        have h₆ : g y = x := hy'
        have h₇ : y = f a := by rw [ha']
        have h₈ : g (f a) = x := by rw [h₇] at h₆; exact h₆
        have h₉ : a = x := by
          calc
            a = g (f a) := by rw [h₅]
            _ = x := by rw [h₈]
        rw [h₉] at ha
        exact ha
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in A, then x is in (image g) (image f A)
        intro hx
        -- Since x is in A, f x is in (image f A) and g (f x) = x
        have h₃ : f x ∈ image f A := by
          refine' ⟨x, hx, _⟩
          <;> simp
        have h₄ : g (f x) = x := by
          have h₄₁ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          simpa using h₄₁
        -- Therefore, x is in (image g) (image f A)
        have h₅ : x ∈ (image g) (image f A) := by
          refine' ⟨f x, h₃, _⟩
          rw [h₄]
        exact h₅
    -- Using the above equivalence, we can conclude that (image g) (image f A) = A
    ext x
    exact h₂ x
  -- Extend the result to any set to show that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id
  funext A
  apply h₁

by
  have h_main : ∀ (X : Set U), (image g) (image f X) = X := by
    intro X
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove (image g) (image f X) ⊆ X
      intro y hy
      rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
      -- z ∈ image f X means ∃ x ∈ X, f x = z
      rcases hz with ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
      -- Now y = g (f x) and g ∘ f = id
      have h2 : g (f x) = x := by
        have h3 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h3
      -- So y = x ∈ X
      rw [h2]
      exact hx
    · -- Prove X ⊆ (image g) (image f X)
      intro x hx
      -- x ∈ X implies f x ∈ image f X
      have h2 : f x ∈ image f X := by
        exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
      -- And g (f x) = x, so x ∈ image g (image f X)
      have h3 : g (f x) = x := by
        have h4 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h4
      refine' ⟨f x, h2, _⟩
      rw [h3]
  -- Now extend to all sets
  funext X
  apply h_main
